Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails using search_emails with query "Mosaic" or "Tamara" to locate the trigger email from Tamara Byrd (email_id: email_tamara_vendor_review_warning_2026_0424, subject: "Need written summary of all open Mosaic matters before Monday vendor review"). The email is in Emeka's INBOX, sent by tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com, CC'd to rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com. It requests a matter-by-matter summary covering the Zara damage claim, Deshawn and Keiko's move status, and Andre's expense hold, with a Saturday deadline ahead of Monday's vendor review meeting.
Search emails using search_emails with query "prototype" or "incident report" or "Zara Okoye" to find the formal incident report email (email_id: mosaic_incident_report_final_001_local) sent by emeka.diallo@moveops.io to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com and rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com, with CC to chloe.vance@moveops.io. This email documents the $90,000 prototype value, $50,000 carrier coverage cap, and $40,000 uncovered gap. Also, find Marcus Thorne's email to Emeka (email_id: email_mosaic_keiko_dot_exposure_2026_0422) confirming the $50,000 PHMSA contingent liability booked for Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187 related to Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter shipment.
Search emails using search_emails with query "Andre" or "stipend" or "reimbursement" to find Marcus Thorne's email to Tamara Byrd (email_id: email_marcus_to_tamara_andre_hold, subject: "Verification needed to release Andre Washington stipend reimbursement"). This shows the $89 reimbursement is on admin hold due to a suspected duplicate receipt, and MoveOps is awaiting written confirmation from Tamara before releasing payment.
Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with query "Heartland" or "prototype" or "Mosaic" to find Chloe Vance's message in #operations (channel C006) reporting that Heartland Movers has gone silent on cost-sharing for the $40,000 uncovered gap, that Chloe is escalating to Lisa Kwan directly, and that MoveOps should prepare to absorb the full $40,000.
Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with query "PHMSA" or "DOT" or "Keiko" or "contingent" to find Marcus Thorne's message in #finance (channel C005) confirming the $50,000 DOT/PHMSA contingent liability booked for Keiko Tanaka's DOT matter under Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187. Marcus notes this is a separate open exposure from the Zara prototype claim and is not part of INV-2026-0411, and advises that anyone summarizing open Mosaic items should include both risks distinctly.
Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill to find the $90,000 damage accrual (bill Id: bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001, DocNumber: ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC) and the $50,000 DOT penalty accrual (bill Id: bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2, DocNumber: ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001). Also find Andre Washington's stipend bill on admin hold (bill Id: bill_stipend_washington_keyboard_hold, DocNumber: STIP-2026-0094, amount $89, status: ADMIN HOLD with duplicate receipt hash flag).
Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_read_invoice to find the Mosaic April invoice (Id: inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc, DocNumber: INV-2026-0411, total $9,010) and the credit memo (Id: cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001, DocNumber: CM-2026-0415, amount -$90,000 pending insurance adjudication).
Search Airtable records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records with table tblRelocations01 and query for "Deshawn Hartley" or "Mosaic" to find relocation records for all three April Mosaic moves. Deshawn Hartley's Tampa-to-Denver record (recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver) shows Status "In Progress" with Move Start Date April 14 and Move End Date April 19 (the hard delivery deadline ahead of Automate 2026 booth setup on April 20). The CRM engagement eng_mosaic_deshawn_completion_2026_0418 confirms actual completion on April 18. Zara Okoye's record (recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta) shows Status "Completed". Keiko Tanaka's record (recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle) shows Status "In Progress".
Search CRM engagements or deals using crm_list_engagements or crm_search_deals with query "Mosaic" to find the existing engagement notes: the damage incident report engagement (eng_mosaic_prototype_incident_report_2026_0415), the DOT exposure engagement (eng_mosaic_keiko_dot_exposure_2026_0422), the Andre stipend hold engagement (eng_mosaic_andre_stipend_hold_2026_0422), and the Deshawn completion engagement (eng_mosaic_deshawn_completion_2026_0418). Also find the at-risk deal (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk) documenting the full damage claim details, including the $40,000 insurance gap.
Send an email (via send_email or reply_to_email) to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com with CC to rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com. The email must be a matter-by-matter summary covering: (1) Zara Okoye damage claim, prototype valued at $90,000, carrier coverage $50,000, uncovered gap $40,000, Heartland recovery in progress but not responding, (2) Deshawn Hartley relocation, completed successfully on April 18 ahead of the April 19 Automate deadline, (3) Keiko Tanaka DOT/PHMSA exposure, $50,000 contingent liability for Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, (4) Andre Washington stipend, $89 on admin hold pending receipt verification from Tamara. Each item should include status, who is handling it, financial exposure, and the next step.
Update the Mosaic CRM deal (via crm_update_deal_stage or crm_create_engagement) to reflect the current at-risk account status so David Chen has visibility into the situation when checking the pipeline.
Post a message in Slack (via conversations_add_message) to #operations (channel C006) addressed to Chloe Vance, letting her know that Mosaic's VP of Engineering has scheduled a Monday vendor review meeting with MoveOps on the agenda, and that Emeka needs her latest on the Heartland cost-sharing situation before Monday.
Create a calendar event (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for Monday, April 27, morning, for a prep call before the Mosaic vendor review meeting. Attendees should include Emeka Diallo (emeka.diallo@moveops.com), Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com), and Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 The email sent to Tamara Byrd includes information about the Zara Okoye prototype damage claim, specifically referencing a prototype value of $90,000 and an uncovered gap of $40,000 after carrier coverage of $50,000.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Tamara explicitly states 'the $90,000 robotic arm', 'vendor liability coverage caps at $50,000 per item', and 'leaves a $40,000 gap' — all three figures are present.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states: 'Prototype Unit #1 ($90,000 value)...a $40,000 uncovered gap' and 'MoveOps' vendor liability coverage caps at $50,000 per item, leaving a $40,000 uncovered gap.' All three dollar figures are present.
✓Run #3The email body explicitly states '$50,000 per-item vendor liability coverage against the $90,000 unit value' and references the '$40,000 insurance shortfall' / '$40,000 uncovered gap'. All three dollar amounts are present.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly states 'Prototype Unit #1 ($90,000 value)', 'MoveOps' vendor liability coverage caps at $50,000 per item', and 'leaving a $40,000 uncovered gap'. All three figures are clearly present.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly states: 'our vendor liability policy covers $50,000 per item. With the prototype valued at $90,000, there is a $40,000 gap.' All three figures are present.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly states 'our vendor liability insurance covers $50,000 per item', 'The damaged unit is valued at $90,000', and 'leaving a $40,000 uncovered gap'. All three figures are present.
3 The email sent to Tamara Byrd includes information about Deshawn Hartley's relocation being completed successfully, referencing delivery by April 18 or ahead of the April 19 Automate 2026 deadline.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Tamara states 'Deshawn's move was delivered April 18, ahead of the Automate 2026 trade show deadline.' Both the date and its significance relative to the deadline are mentioned.
✓Run #2The email states: 'Deshawn's move was picked up April 14, delivered April 18 — one day ahead of his firm April 19 deadline for the Automate 2026 trade show.' Both the April 18 delivery date and the April 19 deadline are explicitly included.
✓Run #3The email states 'Deshawn's move from Tampa to Denver was completed on April 18, one day ahead of his April 19 hard deadline' and references the Automate 2026 deadline explicitly.
✓Run #4The email explicitly states 'delivery was completed April 18 — one day ahead of the hard April 19 deadline for the Automate 2026 trade show'. Both the date and the deadline are referenced.

✓Run #6The email to Tamara explicitly states 'delivered on April 18, and completed one full day ahead of his April 19 hard deadline for the Automate 2026 trade show'.
4 The email sent to Tamara Byrd includes information about the Keiko Tanaka DOT/PHMSA compliance exposure, referencing a contingent liability of $50,000 or Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Tamara references 'PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187' and states 'Current financial exposure is a $50,000 contingent liability', meeting both evidence requirements.
✓Run #2The email includes: 'Marcus Thorne has booked a $50,000 contingent liability on our books (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) as a precaution.' Both the $50K amount and the PHMSA case number are present.
✓Run #3The email explicitly references 'DOT Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187' and a '$50,000 contingent liability booked' for Keiko Tanaka's DOT/PHMSA matter.
✓Run #4The email mentions 'Marcus Thorne, has booked a $50,000 contingent liability for this matter under DOT Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187'. Both the $50K liability and the specific case number are included.
✓Run #5The email includes information about the DOT/PHMSA matter with explicit mention of '$50,000 contingent liability' and 'PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187' in section 3 on Keiko Tanaka.
✓Run #6The email to Tamara includes both the $50,000 contingent liability and PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187 in the Keiko Tanaka section: 'Marcus Thorne, has booked a $50,000 contingent liability under PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187'.
5 The email sent to Tamara Byrd includes information about Andre Washington's stipend reimbursement being on hold for $89 due to a duplicate receipt issue, and that Tamara needs to provide written confirmation to release it.
outcome
